---
title: "Non Parametric Project"
author: "Simran Mallik, Yuritzy Ramos, Melika Baghi,
#          Keerthana Ramasubramonian"
date: "2026-04-13"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

################################################################################
# ISyE 6404 - Internet Access and Female Economic Empowerment: A Cross-National Nonparametric Analysis
# Authors: Simran Mallik, Yuritzy Ramos, Melika Baghi,Keerthana Ramasubramonian
# Data Source: World Bank Gender Data Portal (2022)
################################################################################

```{r}
#Install and Load Required Packages
required_packages <- c("dplyr", "tidyr", "ggplot2", "nortest", "gridExtra")
new_packages <- required_packages[!(required_packages %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"])]
if (length(new_packages)) install.packages(new_packages, repos = "https://cran.r-project.org")

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(nortest)
library(gridExtra)


#Load and Prepare Data
cat("LOADING AND PREPARING DATA")

#Read the three CSV datasets (update paths as needed)
internet <- read.csv("Individuals using the Internet (percent of population).csv",
                      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
employment <- read.csv("Employment to population ratio (percent).csv",
                        stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
management <- read.csv("Female share of employment in senior and middle management (percent).csv",
                        stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

#World Bank aggregate/region codes to exclude (not individual countries)
aggregate_codes <- c("AFE", "AFW", "ARB", "CEB", "CSS", "EAP", "EAR", "EAS",
                     "ECA", "ECS", "EMU", "EUU", "FCS", "HIC", "HPC", "IBD",
                     "IBT", "IDA", "IDB", "IDX", "INX", "LAC", "LCN", "LDC",
                     "LIC", "LMC", "LMY", "LTE", "MEA", "MIC", "MNA", "NAC",
                     "OED", "OSS", "PRE", "PSS", "PST", "SAS", "SSA", "SSF",
                     "SST", "TEA", "TEC", "TLA", "TMN", "TSA", "TSS", "UMC",
                     "WLD")

# Filter internet data for 2022 and keeps observations for "total" disaggregation only rather
# than female or male specifically 
internet_2022 <- internet %>%
  filter(Year == 2022,
         !(Country.Code %in% aggregate_codes),
         grepl("total", Disaggregation, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(Internet = as.numeric(Value)) %>%
  select(Country.Name, Country.Code, Internet) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Internet)) %>%
  distinct(Country.Code, .keep_all = TRUE)

#Filter employment for 2022 and keeps observations for "female, Modeled, 15+" disaggregation
employment_2022 <- employment %>%
  filter(Year == 2022,
         !(Country.Code %in% aggregate_codes),
         grepl("female", Disaggregation, ignore.case = TRUE),
         grepl("Modeled", Disaggregation, ignore.case = TRUE),
         grepl("15\\+", Disaggregation)) %>%
  mutate(Employment = as.numeric(Value)) %>%
  select(Country.Name, Country.Code, Employment) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Employment)) %>%
  distinct(Country.Code, .keep_all = TRUE)

#Filter management for 2022
management_2022 <- management %>%
  filter(Year == 2022,
         !(Country.Code %in% aggregate_codes)) %>%
  mutate(Management = as.numeric(Value)) %>%
  select(Country.Name, Country.Code, Management) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Management)) %>%
  distinct(Country.Code, .keep_all = TRUE)

# cat(sprintf("Unique countries (2022): Internet=%d, Employment(female)=%d, Management=%d\n",nrow(internet_2022), nrow(employment_2022), nrow(management_2022)))

```



```{r}
#Merge employment and internet data sets (inner join keeps only countries with both variables)
data_emp <- inner_join(internet_2022, employment_2022,
                       by = c("Country.Name", "Country.Code")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Internet), !is.na(Employment))

#Merge internet and management data sets 
data_mgmt <- inner_join(internet_2022, management_2022,
                        by = c("Country.Name", "Country.Code")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Internet), !is.na(Management))

#Count how many rows we have matched in each dataset
cat(sprintf("Merged: Internet + Employment = %d countries\n", nrow(data_emp)))
cat(sprintf("Merged: Internet + Management = %d countries\n\n", nrow(data_mgmt)))

#Record how many rows we saw:
# data_emp has 170 rows
# data_mgmt has 93 rows
```

```{r}
#Classify Countries by Internet Access
cat("CLASSIFY INTO HIGH/LOW INTERNET ACCESS GROUPS\n")
#Threshold: 50% (consistent with ITU global average, 2019)

data_emp$Group <- ifelse(data_emp$Internet >= 50,
                         "High Internet (>=50%)",
                         "Low Internet (<50%)")
data_mgmt$Group <- ifelse(data_mgmt$Internet >= 50,
                          "High Internet (>=50%)",
                          "Low Internet (<50%)")

cat("Employment dataset group sizes:\n")
print(table(data_emp$Group))
cat("\nManagement dataset group sizes:\n")
print(table(data_mgmt$Group))

```

```{r}
#Descriptive Statistics 
cat("DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS\n\n")
#Produce the summary statistics for female employment by internet access group (high vs. low)

cat("Female Employment Rate (%) by Internet Access Group:\n")
desc_emp <- data_emp %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    Mean = round(mean(Employment), 2),
    Median = round(median(Employment), 2),
    SD = round(sd(Employment), 2),
    Min = round(min(Employment), 2),
    Max = round(max(Employment), 2),
    IQR = round(IQR(Employment), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
print(as.data.frame(desc_emp))
```



```{r}
cat("\nFemale Share of Senior/Middle Management (%) by Internet Access Group:\n")
desc_mgmt <- data_mgmt %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    Mean = round(mean(Management), 2),
    Median = round(median(Management), 2),
    SD = round(sd(Management), 2),
    Min = round(min(Management), 2),
    Max = round(max(Management), 2),
    IQR = round(IQR(Management), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
print(as.data.frame(desc_mgmt))
cat("\n")
```

```{r}
#Normality Testing
cat("NORMALITY ASSESSMENT \n\n")

# Split data into groups based on low or high internet access
emp_high <- data_emp$Employment[data_emp$Group == "High Internet (>=50%)"]
emp_low  <- data_emp$Employment[data_emp$Group == "Low Internet (<50%)"]
mgmt_high <- data_mgmt$Management[data_mgmt$Group == "High Internet (>=50%)"]
mgmt_low  <- data_mgmt$Management[data_mgmt$Group == "Low Internet (<50%)"]

#Shapiro-Wilk Test
cat("Shapiro-Wilk Test (H0: data is normally distributed; reject if p < 0.05)\n\n")

#Run a Shapiro-Wilk Test for each split of the data
sw_emp_high <- shapiro.test(emp_high)
sw_emp_low  <- shapiro.test(emp_low)
sw_mgmt_high <- shapiro.test(mgmt_high)
sw_mgmt_low  <- shapiro.test(mgmt_low)

#Check the results from the Shapiro-Wilk Test 
cat(sprintf("  Employment - High Internet (n=%d): W=%.4f, p=%.4f  %s\n",
            length(emp_high), sw_emp_high$statistic, sw_emp_high$p.value,
            ifelse(sw_emp_high$p.value < 0.05, "→ REJECT", "→ Fail to reject")))
cat(sprintf("  Employment - Low Internet  (n=%d): W=%.4f, p=%.4f  %s\n",
            length(emp_low), sw_emp_low$statistic, sw_emp_low$p.value,
            ifelse(sw_emp_low$p.value < 0.05, "→ REJECT", "→ Fail to reject")))
cat(sprintf("  Management - High Internet (n=%d): W=%.4f, p=%.4f  %s\n",
            length(mgmt_high), sw_mgmt_high$statistic, sw_mgmt_high$p.value,
            ifelse(sw_mgmt_high$p.value < 0.05, "→ REJECT", "→ Fail to reject")))
cat(sprintf("  Management - Low Internet  (n=%d): W=%.4f, p=%.4f  %s\n\n",
            length(mgmt_low), sw_mgmt_low$statistic, sw_mgmt_low$p.value,
            ifelse(sw_mgmt_low$p.value < 0.05, "→ REJECT", "→ Fail to reject")))

## 3 of the data splits failed to reject the null hypothesis and 1 data split rejected the null hypothesis (non-normal distribution)

```

```{r}
#Anderson-Darling Test 
cat("Anderson-Darling Test\n\n")

#Here our hypothesis is H0: data is normally distributed; reject if p < 0.05
ad_emp_high <- ad.test(emp_high)
ad_emp_low  <- ad.test(emp_low)
ad_mgmt_high <- ad.test(mgmt_high)
ad_mgmt_low  <- ad.test(mgmt_low)

#Print the results for the 4 data splits
cat(sprintf("  Employment - High Internet (n=%d): A=%.4f, p=%.4f  %s\n",
            length(emp_high), ad_emp_high$statistic, ad_emp_high$p.value,
            ifelse(ad_emp_high$p.value < 0.05, "→ REJECT", "→ Fail to reject")))
cat(sprintf("  Employment - Low Internet  (n=%d): A=%.4f, p=%.4f  %s\n",
            length(emp_low), ad_emp_low$statistic, ad_emp_low$p.value,
            ifelse(ad_emp_low$p.value < 0.05, "→ REJECT", "→ Fail to reject")))
cat(sprintf("  Management - High Internet (n=%d): A=%.4f, p=%.4f  %s\n",
            length(mgmt_high), ad_mgmt_high$statistic, ad_mgmt_high$p.value,
            ifelse(ad_mgmt_high$p.value < 0.05, "→ REJECT", "→ Fail to reject")))
cat(sprintf("  Management - Low Internet  (n=%d): A=%.4f, p=%.4f  %s\n\n",
            length(mgmt_low), ad_mgmt_low$statistic, ad_mgmt_low$p.value,
            ifelse(ad_mgmt_low$p.value < 0.05, "→ REJECT", "→ Fail to reject")))

## The same 3 splits of the data failed to reject the null hypothesis and the same split for employment with high internet access rejected the null hypothesis (non-normal distribution)


```

```{r}

#Visual Diagnostics
cat("VISUAL DIAGNOSTICS OF DATA\n\n")

#Figure 1: Histograms with Median Lines
p1 <- ggplot(data_emp, aes(x = Employment, fill = Group)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 12, alpha = 0.7, color = "white") +
  geom_vline(data = data_emp %>% group_by(Group) %>%
               summarise(med = median(Employment), .groups = "drop"),
             aes(xintercept = med), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~Group, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Female Employment Rate by Internet Access Group",
       x = "Female Employment to Population Ratio (%)",
       y = "Frequency") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("High Internet (>=50%)" = "#2166AC",
                                "Low Internet (<50%)" = "#B2182B")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

p2 <- ggplot(data_mgmt, aes(x = Management, fill = Group)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 12, alpha = 0.7, color = "white") +
  geom_vline(data = data_mgmt %>% group_by(Group) %>%
               summarise(med = median(Management), .groups = "drop"),
             aes(xintercept = med), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~Group, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Female Share of Sr/Mid Management by Internet Access Group",
       x = "Female Share of Employment in Senior & Middle Management (%)",
       y = "Frequency") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("High Internet (>=50%)" = "#2166AC",
                                "Low Internet (<50%)" = "#B2182B")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 1)

#Figure 2: QQ-Plots - double checking our normality results
par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))
qqnorm(emp_high, main = paste0("Q-Q Plot: Employment\nHigh Internet (n=",
       length(emp_high), ")"), col = "#2166AC", pch = 16)
qqline(emp_high, col = "red", lwd = 2)
qqnorm(emp_low, main = paste0("Q-Q Plot: Employment\nLow Internet (n=",
       length(emp_low), ")"), col = "#B2182B", pch = 16)
qqline(emp_low, col = "red", lwd = 2)
qqnorm(mgmt_high, main = paste0("Q-Q Plot: Management\nHigh Internet (n=",
       length(mgmt_high), ")"), col = "#2166AC", pch = 16)
qqline(mgmt_high, col = "red", lwd = 2)
qqnorm(mgmt_low, main = paste0("Q-Q Plot: Management\nLow Internet (n=",
       length(mgmt_low), ")"), col = "#B2182B", pch = 16)
qqline(mgmt_low, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# Box Plots Side by Side
p3 <- ggplot(data_emp, aes(x = Group, y = Employment, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.color = "red", outlier.size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Female Employment Rate\nby Internet Access Group",
       x = "", y = "Female Employment to Population Ratio (%)") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("High Internet (>=50%)" = "#2166AC",
                                "Low Internet (<50%)" = "#B2182B")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

p4 <- ggplot(data_mgmt, aes(x = Group, y = Management, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.color = "red", outlier.size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Female Share of Sr/Mid Management\nby Internet Access Group",
       x = "", y = "Female Share of Sr/Mid Management (%)") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("High Internet (>=50%)" = "#2166AC",
                                "Low Internet (<50%)" = "#B2182B")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

grid.arrange(p3, p4, ncol = 2)


```


```{r}
#Mann-Whitney U Tests
cat("MANN-WHITNEY U TESTS\n")
# Use a Bonferroni-corrected alpha = 0.025 per test

#Test 1: Employment
cat("TEST 1: Female Employment Rate ~ Internet Access Group\n")
#Hypothesis 
#H0: No difference in median female employment rate between countries with high vs. low national internet usage
#Ha: The medians differ

#Run the Mann-Whitney Test
mw_emp <- wilcox.test(Employment ~ Group, data = data_emp,
                       alternative = "two.sided", exact = FALSE, correct = TRUE)

#Print the results
cat(sprintf("  W statistic: %.1f\n", mw_emp$statistic))
cat(sprintf("  p-value:     %.6f\n", mw_emp$p.value))
cat(sprintf("  Decision (alpha = 0.025): %s H0\n\n",
            ifelse(mw_emp$p.value < 0.025, "*** REJECT ***", "FAIL TO REJECT"))) #State whether we reject null hypothesis or not

# Determine the effect size: r = |Z| / sqrt(N)
z_emp <- qnorm(mw_emp$p.value / 2)
r_emp <- abs(z_emp) / sqrt(nrow(data_emp))

#Report effect size
cat(sprintf("  Effect size r = |Z|/sqrt(N): %.4f (%s)\n",
            r_emp,
            ifelse(r_emp < 0.1, "negligible",
                   ifelse(r_emp < 0.3, "small",
                          ifelse(r_emp < 0.5, "medium", "large")))))

#Print medians of the high and low internet access groups
cat(sprintf("  Median (High Internet): %.2f%%\n", median(emp_high)))
cat(sprintf("  Median (Low Internet):  %.2f%%\n\n", median(emp_low)))

#Test 2: Management
cat("TEST 2: Female Share of Sr/Mid Management ~ Internet Access Group\n")
#H0: No difference in median female management share between countries with high vs. low national internet usage
#Ha: The medians differ

#Run Mann Whitney Test
mw_mgmt <- wilcox.test(Management ~ Group, data = data_mgmt,
                         alternative = "two.sided", exact = FALSE, correct = TRUE)
#Report the results
cat(sprintf("  W statistic: %.1f\n", mw_mgmt$statistic))
cat(sprintf("  p-value:     %.6f\n", mw_mgmt$p.value))
cat(sprintf("  Decision (alpha = 0.025): %s H0\n\n",
            ifelse(mw_mgmt$p.value < 0.025, "*** REJECT ***", "FAIL TO REJECT"))) #State if we reject null or not

##Determine effect size
z_mgmt <- qnorm(mw_mgmt$p.value / 2)
r_mgmt <- abs(z_mgmt) / sqrt(nrow(data_mgmt))

#Report effect size
cat(sprintf("  Effect size r = |Z|/sqrt(N): %.4f (%s)\n",
            r_mgmt,
            ifelse(r_mgmt < 0.1, "negligible",
                   ifelse(r_mgmt < 0.3, "small",
                          ifelse(r_mgmt < 0.5, "medium", "large")))))

#Print medians of the high and low internet access groups
cat(sprintf("  Median (High Internet): %.2f%%\n", median(mgmt_high)))
cat(sprintf("  Median (Low Internet):  %.2f%%\n\n", median(mgmt_low)))

```

```{r}

#Summary of Findings
#Compile all the findings here

cat("=================================================================\n")
cat("                    SUMMARY OF FINDINGS\n")
cat("=================================================================\n\n")

cat("1. NORMALITY ASSESSMENT:\n")
cat("   Shapiro-Wilk and Anderson-Darling tests, along with histogram\n")
cat("   and Q-Q plot diagnostics, indicate departures from normality\n")
cat("   in at least some groups, justifying the use of nonparametric\n")
cat("   Mann-Whitney U tests.\n\n")

cat("2. TEST 1 — Female Employment Rate:\n")
cat(sprintf("   Median (High Internet): %.2f%%\n", median(emp_high)))
cat(sprintf("   Median (Low Internet):  %.2f%%\n", median(emp_low)))
cat(sprintf("   Mann-Whitney p-value:   %.6f\n", mw_emp$p.value))
cat(sprintf("   Decision: %s H0 at Bonferroni alpha = 0.025\n",
            ifelse(mw_emp$p.value < 0.025, "REJECT", "FAIL TO REJECT")))
cat("   → Countries with LOW internet access tend to have HIGHER female\n")
cat("     employment rates, likely reflecting informal/agricultural labor.\n\n")

cat("3. TEST 2 — Female Share of Senior/Middle Management:\n")
cat(sprintf("   Median (High Internet): %.2f%%\n", median(mgmt_high)))
cat(sprintf("   Median (Low Internet):  %.2f%%\n", median(mgmt_low)))
cat(sprintf("   Mann-Whitney p-value:   %.6f\n", mw_mgmt$p.value))
cat(sprintf("   Decision: %s H0 at Bonferroni alpha = 0.025\n",
            ifelse(mw_mgmt$p.value < 0.025, "REJECT", "FAIL TO REJECT")))
cat("   → Countries with HIGH internet access have significantly HIGHER\n")
cat("     female representation in senior/middle management.\n\n")

cat("4. INTERPRETATION:\n")
cat("   While higher internet access is associated with greater female\n")
cat("   representation in management, the relationship with overall\n")
cat("   employment is more nuanced. Low-internet countries may show\n")
cat("   higher female employment due to informal and agricultural labor.\n\n")
cat("   This analysis is cross-sectional and correlational. No causal\n")
cat("   claims are made. Confounding factors such as regional cultural\n")
cat("   norms, economic development level, and labor market policies\n")
cat("   may influence both internet access and female economic outcomes.\n")
cat("=================================================================\n")
```
